member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Abstractions.Contracts.IAuthFailureHandler
member.summary: Handles authentication failure events. Bridge plugins use this to propagate auth failures to the host for UI notification.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Abstractions.Contracts.IAuthFailureHandler.Status
member.summary: Gets the current authentication status.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Abstractions.Contracts.IAuthFailureHandler.HandleFailureAsync(Lidarr.Plugin.Abstractions.Contracts.AuthFailure,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles an authentication failure event.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Abstractions.Contracts.IAuthFailureHandler.HandleSuccessAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles successful authentication.
member.remarks: IMPLEMENTATIONS MUST BE IDEMPOTENT. Callers (e.g. AuthFailureDelegatingHandler) may invoke this method on every successful response while the handler is in a non-healthy state, and concurrent probes during recovery may produce overlapping calls. The handler must not produce externally-visible side effects beyond the first invocation per recovery (no double-counting metrics, no duplicate event emission, no repeated DB writes).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Abstractions.Contracts.IAuthFailureHandler.RequestReauthenticationAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Requests re-authentication from the user.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Abstractions.Contracts.IAuthFailureHandler.LastFailure
member.summary: Most recent failure details, or null if no failure has been observed since the last . Implementers SHOULD expose this so consumers (e.g. AuthFailureGate) can surface actionable error messages without downcasting to a specific implementation. Default returns null for backwards compatibility with handlers that haven't been updated.

member.remarks: RSS support may not be feasible for all bridge plugins due to architectural constraints. The native RSS path via HttpIndexerBase remains available for plugins that require it.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Diagnostics.DiagnosticHealthResult
member.summary: Represents the result of a general-purpose provider health check. Unlike (which is LLM-specific and includes a Model field), this type uses and fields appropriate for non-LLM providers such as streaming services that have no "model" concept.

remarks.para: Streaming plugins (Qobuzarr, Tidalarr, AppleMusicarr) should use this type instead of to avoid semantic confusion where Model = "quality_detect" implies an LLM model identifier.
Use and to convert between this type and when interoperability is needed.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Diagnostics.DiagnosticHealthResult.IsHealthy
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the provider is healthy and ready.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Diagnostics.DiagnosticHealthResult.StatusMessage
member.summary: Gets an optional message describing the health status.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Diagnostics.DiagnosticHealthResult.ResponseTime
member.summary: Gets the time taken to perform the health check.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Diagnostics.DiagnosticHealthResult.Provider
member.summary: Gets the name/identifier of the provider (e.g., 'tidal', 'qobuz', 'apple-music').

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Diagnostics.DiagnosticHealthResult.AuthMethod
member.summary: Gets the authentication method used by the provider.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Diagnostics.DiagnosticHealthResult.DiagnosticType
member.summary: Gets the type of diagnostic check performed. Replaces the LLM-specific Model field with a semantically correct identifier for non-LLM providers.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Diagnostics.DiagnosticHealthResult.Capability
member.summary: Gets the capability that was checked. Describes what the provider can do when healthy.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Diagnostics.DiagnosticHealthResult.ErrorCode
member.summary: Gets the error code if the health check failed.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ILlmProvider
member.summary: Core abstraction for LLM providers. Focused on raw LLM operations, not domain-specific tasks like recommendation generation. Inspired by Microsoft.Extensions.AI patterns.

remarks.para: This interface provides a unified way to interact with various LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, local models, etc.) through a common contract.
Implementations should handle: - Authentication and API key management - Rate limiting and retry logic - Error mapping to normalized exceptions - Logging with proper redaction of sensitive data
For domain-specific operations (e.g., music recommendations), create adapter classes that use ILlmProvider internally but expose domain-specific methods.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ILlmProvider.ProviderId
member.summary: Gets the unique identifier for this provider. Used for logging, configuration, and provider selection.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ILlmProvider.DisplayName
member.summary: Gets the human-readable display name for this provider. Suitable for display in user interfaces.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ILlmProvider.Capabilities
member.summary: Gets the capabilities this provider supports. Use this to check for features before attempting to use them.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ILlmProvider.CheckHealthAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Checks if the provider is ready to accept requests. Verifies authentication, connectivity, and service availability.
member.param: Token to cancel the health check.
member.returns: A indicating whether the provider is healthy.
member.remarks: Implementations should: - Verify API key/credentials are valid - Check if the service endpoint is reachable - Return quickly (typically under 5 seconds) - Not throw exceptions; return unhealthy result instead

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ILlmProvider.CompleteAsync(Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Completes a prompt and returns the full response (non-streaming).
member.param: The request containing the prompt and configuration.
member.returns: The complete response from the LLM.
member.remarks: Implementations may throw provider-specific exceptions for authentication failures, rate limiting, or network/provider errors. In Lidarr.Plugin.Common, these are represented by exceptions in the Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Errors namespace.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ILlmProvider.StreamAsync(Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Streams a prompt completion, returning chunks as they arrive.
member.param: The request containing the prompt and configuration.
member.returns: An async enumerable of response chunks, or null if streaming is not supported.

remarks.para: Returns null (not an empty enumerable) if the provider does not support streaming. Check . for before calling this method.
The return type is nullable rather than because providers that don't support streaming can return null synchronously without awaiting.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmCapabilityFlags
member.summary: Flags indicating the capabilities supported by an LLM provider. These flags enable capability detection for intelligent feature usage.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmCapabilityFlags.None
member.summary: No capabilities. Default value.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmCapabilityFlags.TextCompletion
member.summary: Provider supports basic text completion (prompt in, text out). This is the most fundamental capability that all providers should support.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmCapabilityFlags.Streaming
member.summary: Provider supports streaming responses via Server-Sent Events or similar. When set, returns a valid async enumerable.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmCapabilityFlags.JsonMode
member.summary: Provider supports JSON mode for structured output. When set, the provider can be instructed to output valid JSON.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmCapabilityFlags.SystemPrompt
member.summary: Provider supports system prompts for behavior configuration. When set, is honored.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmCapabilityFlags.ToolCalling
member.summary: Provider supports function/tool calling capabilities. Enables agentic workflows with tool use.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmCapabilityFlags.Vision
member.summary: Provider supports vision/image input. Enables multimodal prompts with images.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmCapabilityFlags.ExtendedThinking
member.summary: Provider supports extended thinking or reasoning modes. Examples: Claude's thinking mode, GLM's reasoning capabilities. When set, may be populated.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmCapabilityFlags.TokenCounting
member.summary: Provider can accurately count or estimate tokens. Enables pre-request token estimation for context management.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmProviderCapabilities
member.summary: Describes the capabilities and constraints of an LLM provider. Used for capability detection and intelligent provider selection.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmProviderCapabilities.Flags
member.summary: Gets the capability flags indicating which features this provider supports. Use bitwise operations to check for specific capabilities.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmProviderCapabilities.MaxContextTokens
member.summary: Gets the maximum context window size in tokens, if known. Null indicates the limit is unknown or unlimited.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmProviderCapabilities.MaxOutputTokens
member.summary: Gets the maximum output tokens the provider can generate in a single response. Null indicates the limit is unknown or unlimited.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmProviderCapabilities.SupportedModels
member.summary: Gets the list of model identifiers supported by this provider. Empty list indicates the provider accepts any model string or has a single default model.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmProviderCapabilities.UsesOpenAiCompatibleApi
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this provider uses an OpenAI-compatible API. When true, the provider can potentially be used with OpenAI-compatible clients.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest
member.summary: Represents a request to an LLM provider for text completion. This is a provider-agnostic data contract that works with any ILlmProvider implementation.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.Prompt
member.summary: Gets the user prompt to send to the LLM. This is the primary input text that the model will respond to.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.SystemPrompt
member.summary: Gets the optional system prompt for configuring model behavior. System prompts typically set the persona, tone, or constraints for responses. Only honored when provider has capability.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.Model
member.summary: Gets the optional model identifier to use for this request. When null, the provider's default model is used.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.Temperature
member.summary: Gets the optional sampling temperature for response generation. Typically ranges from 0.0 (deterministic) to 2.0 (creative). When null, the provider's default temperature is used.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.MaxTokens
member.summary: Gets the optional maximum number of tokens to generate in the response. When null, the provider's default or maximum is used.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.Timeout
member.summary: Gets the optional timeout for this specific request. Overrides any default timeout configured on the provider.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.JsonMode
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the provider should be instructed to return a strict JSON response (where supported). Each provider encodes this differently in its native protocol (for example OpenAI's response_format=json_object, Anthropic's prompt-conditioned JSON shaping, Gemini's responseMimeType, or Z.AI's OpenAI-compatible response_format) so the unified abstraction surfaces the intent here and lets each provider translate it.
member.remarks: Backward compatible: defaults to false. Providers that do not advertise the capability should ignore this flag rather than fail.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.ProviderOptions
member.summary: Gets optional provider-specific options for advanced configuration. These are passed through to the underlying provider without interpretation. Use for provider-specific features not covered by standard properties.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.Thinking
member.summary: Gets the optional thinking/reasoning hint for providers that support extended-thinking modes (Anthropic, Gemini, etc.). Defaults to , in which case the adapter should not send an explicit thinking directive.

remarks.para: Backward-compatible: when null, providers behave exactly as they did before this property existed. Providers that do not advertise should silently ignore the hint rather than fail.
Source: brainarr Phase 4a feedback — adapters previously had to "sneak" thinking-mode through sentinels (e.g., "claude-sonnet-thinking"); this property lets them carry the intent explicitly.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.Tools
member.summary: Gets the optional set of tools the model is allowed to call on this request. When non-null and non-empty, providers that advertise translate this list into their native tool-declaration wire shape (OpenAI's tools[].function, Anthropic's tools[] with input_schema, Gemini's functionDeclarations, Z.AI's OpenAI-compatible shape). Defaults to .

remarks.para: Backward-compatible: when null, providers behave exactly as they did before this property existed. Providers that do not advertise the capability should silently ignore tools rather than fail.
Source: brainarr Phase 5e P1 — closes the deferred gap where the flag had no shared data shape, forcing tool-using consumers to bypass the adapter.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.ToolChoice
member.summary: Gets the tool-choice strategy that controls whether the model is allowed (or required) to call tools. Providers translate to the vendor's native shape (OpenAI's tool_choice, Anthropic's tool_choice, Gemini's toolConfig.functionCallingConfig.mode). Defaults to , which lets the model decide.
member.remarks: Backward-compatible: matches every vendor's pre-existing default behavior, so callers that do not set this field see no change.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmRequest.ToolResults
member.summary: Gets prior tool outputs the host wants the model to see on this turn. Used in multi-turn agentic workflows: turn N emits , the host runs the tools, and turn N+1 supplies their outputs here. Each matches a previously returned . Defaults to .
member.remarks: Backward-compatible: when null, providers behave exactly as they did before this property existed.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmResponse
member.summary: Represents a response from an LLM provider after text completion. This is a provider-agnostic data contract returned by any ILlmProvider implementation.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmResponse.Content
member.summary: Gets the generated text content from the model. This is the primary output of the completion request.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmResponse.ReasoningContent
member.summary: Gets the reasoning or thinking content for models that support extended thinking. Populated when provider has capability and the model provides separate reasoning output (e.g., Claude's thinking, GLM reasoning).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmResponse.Usage
member.summary: Gets the token usage information for this request/response pair. May be null if the provider doesn't report usage or the capability is not supported.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmResponse.FinishReason
member.summary: Gets the reason why the model stopped generating text. Common values: "stop" (natural completion), "length" (max tokens reached), "content_filter" (safety filter triggered).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmResponse.Metadata
member.summary: Gets optional provider-specific metadata about the response. Contains additional information that varies by provider.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmResponse.ToolCalls
member.summary: Gets the tool calls the model emitted in this response. Populated when the model chose to invoke one or more of the tools supplied via . Each entry carries an that the host echoes back on the corresponding follow-up .

remarks.para: Backward-compatible: defaults to . Callers should check for ToolCalls != null regardless of — different vendors set it differently (OpenAI: "tool_calls"; Anthropic: "tool_use"; Gemini may keep the OpenAI-style stop reason or omit it entirely) and the abstraction does not normalize the string.
Source: brainarr Phase 5e P1 — closes the deferred tool-calling gap so that providers advertising have a shared data shape to surface model-emitted calls.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmStreamChunk
member.summary: Represents a single chunk in a streaming LLM response. Returned by for providers that support the capability.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmStreamChunk.ContentDelta
member.summary: Gets the incremental content text in this chunk. Concatenate all ContentDelta values to build the complete response. May be null for metadata-only chunks (e.g., final usage report).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmStreamChunk.ReasoningDelta
member.summary: Gets the incremental reasoning text for models that support extended thinking. Populated when provider has capability and is streaming reasoning output.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmStreamChunk.IsComplete
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this is the final chunk in the stream. When true, the stream is complete and no more chunks will follow.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmStreamChunk.FinalUsage
member.summary: Gets the final token usage information, populated only on the last chunk. Check is true before accessing this value.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmThinkingMode
member.summary: Selects how a provider should treat extended-thinking / reasoning hints attached to an .

remarks.para: Source: brainarr Phase 4a feedback ("Anthropic and Gemini both accept thinking/reasoning hints; a common LlmRequest.Thinking { Mode, BudgetTokens } record would let the adapter carry them explicitly instead of sneaking them through request.Model sentinels").

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmThinkingMode.Disabled
member.summary: Do not request thinking. The provider should suppress reasoning when possible (Anthropic thinking.type=disabled, Gemini thinkingBudget=0, OpenAI default).

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmThinkingMode.Auto
member.summary: Let the provider decide. The adapter should not send an explicit thinking directive — the provider's default behavior applies.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmThinkingMode.Enabled
member.summary: Request that the provider perform extended thinking. , when set, hints at a thinking-token budget where supported (Anthropic thinking.budget_tokens, Gemini thinkingConfig.thinkingBudget).

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmThinkingHint
member.summary: Optional thinking/reasoning hint carried on an . Providers translate the hint into their native protocol (Anthropic's thinking, Gemini's thinkingConfig, etc.) and silently ignore it when the underlying model does not advertise .
member.param: How the provider should treat thinking on this request.
Optional thinking-token budget. Hint only — providers that don't support a budget knob ignore this value. Negative values are treated as .

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmThinkingHint.#ctor(Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmThinkingMode,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Optional thinking/reasoning hint carried on an . Providers translate the hint into their native protocol (Anthropic's thinking, Gemini's thinkingConfig, etc.) and silently ignore it when the underlying model does not advertise .
member.param: How the provider should treat thinking on this request.
Optional thinking-token budget. Hint only — providers that don't support a budget knob ignore this value. Negative values are treated as .

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmThinkingHint.Mode
member.summary: How the provider should treat thinking on this request.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmThinkingHint.BudgetTokens
member.summary: Optional thinking-token budget. Hint only — providers that don't support a budget knob ignore this value. Negative values are treated as .

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmTool
member.summary: Vendor-neutral description of a tool the model is permitted to call. Carried on ; each provider translates this into its native wire format (OpenAI's tools[].function, Anthropic's tools[] with input_schema, Gemini's functionDeclarations, Z.AI's OpenAI-compatible shape, etc.).
member.param: Identifier the model uses to invoke the tool. Must match across , , and .
Human-readable description of what the tool does. Steers when the model chooses to call it. Per spec this slot is non-optional — pass when no description is available rather than null.

JSON Schema describing the tool's input parameters. Held as a so the schema can be carried through the pipeline without lossy re-serialization. Providers serialize it directly into the vendor's parameter slot (parameters for OpenAI/Gemini, input_schema for Anthropic).
Tool kind. Defaults to — the only universally-supported shape today; future kinds (web search, code interpreter, etc.) can extend the enum without breaking the contract.

remarks.para: Source: brainarr Phase 5e P1 — providers needed a shared tool-calling shape so the flag (already exposed) could carry data through the abstraction instead of forcing consumers to bypass it.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmTool.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonElement,Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolKind)
member.summary: Vendor-neutral description of a tool the model is permitted to call. Carried on ; each provider translates this into its native wire format (OpenAI's tools[].function, Anthropic's tools[] with input_schema, Gemini's functionDeclarations, Z.AI's OpenAI-compatible shape, etc.).
member.param: Identifier the model uses to invoke the tool. Must match across , , and .
Human-readable description of what the tool does. Steers when the model chooses to call it. Per spec this slot is non-optional — pass when no description is available rather than null.

JSON Schema describing the tool's input parameters. Held as a so the schema can be carried through the pipeline without lossy re-serialization. Providers serialize it directly into the vendor's parameter slot (parameters for OpenAI/Gemini, input_schema for Anthropic).
Tool kind. Defaults to — the only universally-supported shape today; future kinds (web search, code interpreter, etc.) can extend the enum without breaking the contract.

remarks.para: Source: brainarr Phase 5e P1 — providers needed a shared tool-calling shape so the flag (already exposed) could carry data through the abstraction instead of forcing consumers to bypass it.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmTool.Name
member.summary: Identifier the model uses to invoke the tool. Must match across , , and .

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmTool.Description
member.summary: Human-readable description of what the tool does. Steers when the model chooses to call it. Per spec this slot is non-optional — pass when no description is available rather than null.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmTool.ParametersSchema
member.summary: JSON Schema describing the tool's input parameters. Held as a so the schema can be carried through the pipeline without lossy re-serialization. Providers serialize it directly into the vendor's parameter slot (parameters for OpenAI/Gemini, input_schema for Anthropic).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmTool.Kind
member.summary: Tool kind. Defaults to — the only universally-supported shape today; future kinds (web search, code interpreter, etc.) can extend the enum without breaking the contract.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolCall
member.summary: A tool call the model emitted in its response. Surfaces on and is matched back to a follow-up via .
member.param: Vendor-supplied identifier for this call. OpenAI returns it as tool_calls[].id; Anthropic uses the tool_use block id. Gemini does not emit one natively, so the Gemini adapter synthesizes a stable id (e.g. "call_{index}") so that follow-up s can be correlated uniformly. The host MUST echo this id back on the corresponding result.
Name of the tool being invoked. Matches a previously-supplied .
Parsed JSON arguments the model chose. Held as a so the host can access fields directly without an extra re-parse.

Optional pre-parse text exactly as the vendor returned it. Useful for fidelity (e.g. preserving floating-point precision, key ordering, or original JSON whitespace) and as a fallback when couldn't be parsed.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolCall.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonElement,System.String)
member.summary: A tool call the model emitted in its response. Surfaces on and is matched back to a follow-up via .
member.param: Vendor-supplied identifier for this call. OpenAI returns it as tool_calls[].id; Anthropic uses the tool_use block id. Gemini does not emit one natively, so the Gemini adapter synthesizes a stable id (e.g. "call_{index}") so that follow-up s can be correlated uniformly. The host MUST echo this id back on the corresponding result.
Name of the tool being invoked. Matches a previously-supplied .
Parsed JSON arguments the model chose. Held as a so the host can access fields directly without an extra re-parse.

Optional pre-parse text exactly as the vendor returned it. Useful for fidelity (e.g. preserving floating-point precision, key ordering, or original JSON whitespace) and as a fallback when couldn't be parsed.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolCall.Id
member.summary: Vendor-supplied identifier for this call. OpenAI returns it as tool_calls[].id; Anthropic uses the tool_use block id. Gemini does not emit one natively, so the Gemini adapter synthesizes a stable id (e.g. "call_{index}") so that follow-up s can be correlated uniformly. The host MUST echo this id back on the corresponding result.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolCall.Name
member.summary: Name of the tool being invoked. Matches a previously-supplied .

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolCall.Arguments
member.summary: Parsed JSON arguments the model chose. Held as a so the host can access fields directly without an extra re-parse.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolCall.RawArguments
member.summary: Optional pre-parse text exactly as the vendor returned it. Useful for fidelity (e.g. preserving floating-point precision, key ordering, or original JSON whitespace) and as a fallback when couldn't be parsed.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolChoice
member.summary: Controls whether the model is allowed (or required) to call tools on a given request. Carried on ; providers translate to their native shape (OpenAI's tool_choice, Anthropic's tool_choice, Gemini's toolConfig.functionCallingConfig.mode).

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolChoice.Auto
member.summary: Default. The model decides whether to call a tool or respond directly. Maps to OpenAI "auto", Anthropic {"type":"auto"}, Gemini "AUTO".

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolChoice.None
member.summary: Tool calls are disabled even when is non-empty. Maps to OpenAI "none", Anthropic {"type":"none"}, Gemini "NONE".

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolChoice.Required
member.summary: The model MUST call at least one tool from . Maps to OpenAI "required", Anthropic {"type":"any"}, Gemini "ANY".

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolKind
member.summary: Discriminator for the kind of tool an represents. Today only is universally supported across vendor providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Z.AI/GLM); the enum exists so future tool kinds (vendor-hosted web search, code interpreter, file search, etc.) can be added without breaking the abstraction.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolKind.Function
member.summary: A free-form function the host implements. The model chooses arguments matching the tool's ; the host runs the function and returns the result via .

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolResult
member.summary: The host's reply to a previously-emitted . Carried on for the follow-up turn so the model can incorporate the tool's output. Providers translate to the vendor's tool-result shape (OpenAI's role=tool message with tool_call_id, Anthropic's content[].type=tool_result block, Gemini's functionResponse part).
member.param: Identifier of the originating . Required so the model can match this result to the call it emitted.

Serialized tool output the model should see. Typically JSON, but may be a plain string — providers pass this through verbatim. Callers are responsible for any size-limiting or sanitization (the abstraction does not impose a max length).

When , signals the tool failed and the model should treat as an error description rather than a successful result. Maps to OpenAI's lack of explicit flag (errors stringified into output), Anthropic's tool_result.is_error, and Gemini's error-shaped functionResponse.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolResult.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: The host's reply to a previously-emitted . Carried on for the follow-up turn so the model can incorporate the tool's output. Providers translate to the vendor's tool-result shape (OpenAI's role=tool message with tool_call_id, Anthropic's content[].type=tool_result block, Gemini's functionResponse part).
member.param: Identifier of the originating . Required so the model can match this result to the call it emitted.

Serialized tool output the model should see. Typically JSON, but may be a plain string — providers pass this through verbatim. Callers are responsible for any size-limiting or sanitization (the abstraction does not impose a max length).

When , signals the tool failed and the model should treat as an error description rather than a successful result. Maps to OpenAI's lack of explicit flag (errors stringified into output), Anthropic's tool_result.is_error, and Gemini's error-shaped functionResponse.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolResult.ToolCallId
member.summary: Identifier of the originating . Required so the model can match this result to the call it emitted.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolResult.Output
member.summary: Serialized tool output the model should see. Typically JSON, but may be a plain string — providers pass this through verbatim. Callers are responsible for any size-limiting or sanitization (the abstraction does not impose a max length).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmToolResult.IsError
member.summary: When , signals the tool failed and the model should treat as an error description rather than a successful result. Maps to OpenAI's lack of explicit flag (errors stringified into output), Anthropic's tool_result.is_error, and Gemini's error-shaped functionResponse.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmUsage
member.summary: Represents token usage information for an LLM request/response. Used for monitoring, cost estimation, and context window management.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmUsage.InputTokens
member.summary: Gets the number of tokens in the input prompt. Includes both the system prompt and user prompt if applicable.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmUsage.OutputTokens
member.summary: Gets the number of tokens generated in the response.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmUsage.TotalTokens
member.summary: Gets the total number of tokens used (input + output). Useful for context window tracking and cost estimation.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.LlmUsage.EstimatedCostUsd
member.summary: Gets the estimated cost in USD for this request, if calculable. Based on the provider's pricing model and token counts. Null if pricing information is not available.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult
member.summary: Represents the result of an LLM provider health check. Returned by to indicate whether the provider is ready to accept requests.

remarks.para: This record extends the basic health check with DIAG-02 fields that provide additional diagnostic information for troubleshooting provider issues.
All fields are nullable to maintain backward compatibility with existing code that doesn't use the extended fields.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult.IsHealthy
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the provider is healthy and ready. When true, the provider can accept requests. When false, check for details.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult.StatusMessage
member.summary: Gets an optional message describing the health status. Particularly useful for unhealthy or degraded states to explain the issue.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult.ResponseTime
member.summary: Gets the time taken to perform the health check. Useful for monitoring provider latency and performance.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult.Provider
member.summary: Gets the name/identifier of the LLM provider (e.g., 'openai', 'claude-code', 'glm').
member.remarks: This field enables logging and UI to identify which provider failed or returned a specific status. Useful for debugging when multiple providers are configured.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult.AuthMethod
member.summary: Gets the authentication method used by the provider.
member.remarks: Indicates how the provider authenticates with the API (e.g., API key, device code flow, CLI, OAuth, etc.). This helps diagnose authentication-related failures.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult.Model
member.summary: Gets the model identifier used for this health check.
member.remarks: If the provider supports multiple models, this specifies which model was used. Helps correlate health checks with specific model configurations.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult.ErrorCode
member.summary: Gets the error code if the provider check failed.
member.remarks: Provides a standardized error identifier that can be used for: - Automated issue triage and routing - Error message translation - Alert thresholding and monitoring

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult.Healthy(System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a healthy result indicating the provider is ready.
member.param: Optional response time from the health check.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult.HealthyFromStopwatch(System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Creates a healthy result, sourcing the response time from the supplied . When the stopwatch has not been started (its is and it is not running), the response time is reported as null rather than to avoid signalling a misleading "0 ms" latency.
Distinct name (not an overload of ) to satisfy the Roslyn PublicAPI rule "Do not add multiple overloads with optional parameters" — both this method and the TimeSpan-based variant have optional provider/authMethod/model tail params, which would otherwise make Healthy(provider: "...") calls ambiguous.
member.param: Stopwatch capturing the health-check duration. Null treated as no measurement.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult.Unhealthy(System.String,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an unhealthy result indicating the provider cannot accept requests.
member.param: The reason the provider is unhealthy.
Optional response time from the health check.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Llm.ProviderHealthResult.Degraded(System.String,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a degraded result indicating the provider is functional but impaired. Degraded providers can still accept requests but may have reduced performance.
member.param: The reason the provider is degraded.
Optional response time from the health check.
Optional standardized error/warning code for diagnostics (e.g., CONNECTION_REFUSED, STARTUP).
member.returns: A degraded (marked as healthy but with warning message).

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.ConfidenceBand
member.summary: Standardized confidence band classification for triage decisions. Provides a coarse-grained view of confidence levels used by recommendation triage advisors across all plugins.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.ConfidenceBand.High
member.summary: Confidence score >= 0.8 — high certainty, typically auto-acceptable.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.ConfidenceBand.Medium
member.summary: Confidence score >= 0.6 and < 0.8 — moderate certainty, may need review.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.ConfidenceBand.Low
member.summary: Confidence score < 0.6 — low certainty, typically requires manual review.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.TriageReasonCodes
member.summary: Canonical reason code constants for recommendation triage decisions. All plugins performing automated queue triage should use these constants instead of defining local reason code strings to ensure ecosystem-wide parity.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.TriageReasonCodes.ConfidenceBelowThreshold
member.summary: Item confidence score is below the configured minimum threshold.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.TriageReasonCodes.ConfidenceFarBelowThreshold
member.summary: Item confidence score is substantially below the threshold (>= 0.15 gap).

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.TriageReasonCodes.MissingRequiredMbids
member.summary: Required MusicBrainz identifiers (artist/album) are missing.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.TriageReasonCodes.DuplicateSignal
member.summary: Duplicate-like signal detected in recommendation rationale.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.TriageReasonCodes.HighConfidenceWithMbid
member.summary: High confidence score combined with verified artist MBID (risk reduction).

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.TriageReasonCodes.ConsistentSignals
member.summary: All signals are consistent; no risk factors detected.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.TriageReasonCodes.CalibrationApplied
member.summary: Provider calibration adjusted the raw confidence score.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Abstractions.Triage.TriageReasonCodes.LowCalibrationProvider
member.summary: Provider is known to produce lower-quality confidence estimates.
